item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent series of edits by LordBlaze was reverted, as it unnecessarily changed the UK spelling variants of words to the US versions.

As noted in the post above, UK spelling and usage is acceptable on the wiki, as long as it is consistent. Editors should not be making edits to change UK spellings to US without permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: UK spelling and usage is acceptable, as long as it is consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author is evidently from the UK or commonwealth thereof, but should it still be "Neutralised" with an s instead of a z? The way it's spelled universally on this website, from what I've seen, is with a z, so that might make it confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I can see why some might say the murder mystery is not integral enough to the article for the contest, I personally think that making it any more central would have thrown off fair amount of the emotional build up to the piece, and possible have done away with the "mystery" part of the "murder mystery."

As is, we have a lot of people who would have wanted the skip dead, both inside and outside the Foundation, and there is a very good case for all parties. That's a job well done.

+1 MMG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree with the preceding commenters. There is a murder, and it is mysterious. The fact that the contest theme is applied to an SCP that would already hold up well outside of the JamCon is an added bonus to me. A solid skip overall. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm quite satisfied with that (although I do think this is actually more conventional of a murder mystery than some other entries). Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is well-done, and I love all things historical. However, I'm going to have to echo MrWrong's sentiments that it isn't quite a good fit for the contest. Will upvote it once the contest is over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. I would argue nothing says the article has to be entirely based around the theme, and I'm sure many other entries will interpret it flexibly, but if this was disliked for that reason I would have trouble arguing otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice SCP. However, the murder mystery aspect is not that integral to the piece. You still did whatever you can to make that aspect work, but I would say that it's not fully a murder mystery (maybe one by technicality).

item.title: 'The World Is Going Away'
item.content:encoded:
This is my entry for day 1 of the jam contest, theme 'murder mystery'.

Thanks to XilasCrowe for giving feedback in chat.

Image source.
